**The latest data out of Iowa:****Feb. 18 fundraising deadline****is critical to protecting Senate majority**
Peter – radical Republican Sen. Chuck Grassley (IA) is running for reelection, and his challenger, Democrat Abby Finkenauer, is going to need all of the grassroots support possible to defeat him. **Here are the latest numbers from our data team:**
* **64% of likely Iowa voters DON'T want to reelect Sen. Grassley** , and Abby is running to defeat him in what The Des Moines Register called a "marquee matchup" – making this our best chance in decades to flip this seat blue.

* **The Senate is split 50-50** , meaning Democrats MUST defeat vulnerable incumbents like Sen. Grassley to maintain and expand our majority.

* The GOP Senate campaign arm already has $39 MILLION dedicated to defeating Democratic challengers like Abby and taking back the Senate – and an ally of Mitch McConnell has vowed to make Joe Biden a "half-term president" by dismantling Democratic majorities in Congress.

* Our next big fundraising deadline is TONIGHT, and **Abby is forecasted to fall short of the $12,000 she needs to raise to defeat Sen. Grassley in Iowa.**

Peter, Sen. Chuck Grassley is vulnerable, and if Abby defeats him it will virtually guarantee Democrats control of the Senate. However, we simply cannot win without the resources to compete. **[We need a major surge in donations before tonight's fundraising deadline. name: Peter Franchot
subject: Here are the bills I’m supporting this Legislative Session
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/81826/images/FAWlogo_Blue%404x.png]
Dear Peter,
I’m excited to support several important bills currently under consideration by the MDGA this Legislative Session. From legalizing cannabis, to taking bold action on climate change, to banning ghost guns, passing paid family leave, and supporting our small businesses, we have a historic opportunity to deliver bold and meaningful change for Marylanders this year.
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/81826/images/PF_LegislativeAgenda2022_1200x1200_Gradient_2.png]
Here are some highlights from the bills I’m supporting:
HB1: Legalize Cannabis Via Ballot Referendum
In survey after survey, the consensus is clear – the people of Maryland have decided that the criminalization of cannabis is wrong, it is an example of outdated thinking, and it has cost too many people too much to continue any longer. I’m pleased that this bill requires voters to have a say in this important question via ballot referendum, and I’m confident that this issue will be decided favorably by the will of the people. It is imperative that any legal framework for cannabis includes reparative justice, social equity, and community reinvestment from the outset to benefit our Black communities who have been disproportionately affected by cannabis prohibition. That’s why as Governor, I’ll make sure that Maryland’s legal cannabis industry leads the nation in participation for Minority-Owned Businesses.
SB528: Reduce Greenhouse Gas Emissions
Combating climate change should be a top priority in Maryland, and Senate Bill 528 is a step in the right direction by introducing bold changes to the State’s current approach. The bill requires the State to develop plans for a further 20 percent reduction in greenhouse gas emissions from 2006 levels by 2030 and achieve net-zero by 2045. I believe Maryland should become a regional leader by investing in renewable energy to become a net exporter of energy to the interstate grid, and Senate Bill 528 will ensure meaningful progress.
HB10/SB61: Justly Transition MTA workers to Zero-Emission Buses
During the 2021 regular General Assembly Session the Legislature took decisive action to require that the Maryland Transit Administration (MTA) transition to zero-emission buses. This bill works to ensure a just transition for employees accustomed to operating and maintaining diesel buses and associated infrastructure. It is crucial to the well-being of MTA employees that they receive the necessary safety and workforce development training to perform their jobs safely and effectively.
HB425/SB387: Ban Ghost Guns
In Maryland, we lose approximately 500 of our fellow citizens every year to gun-related violence – a toll that falls disproportionately on vulnerable communities and people of color. Despite these alarming statistics, there are still nationwide efforts in both policy, and technology development to make access to firearms easier. The only parties who stand to benefit from the philosophy that ‘more guns mean less crime’ are the firearm manufacturers who have a financial interest in the continued legality of these so-called ‘ghost guns.’ These unserialized firearms are increasingly available, and more frequently used in criminal activity. It is crucial that this bill is enacted into law to help keep these essentially untraceable and uncontrollable weapons off the streets.
HB8/SB275: Pass Paid Family and Medical Leave
One of the many inequities that the COVID-19 pandemic has revealed is our country’s lack of paid family and medical leave policies. This bill is vitally important to correct that inequity and ensure that workers can care for themselves and loved ones in times of illness without jeopardizing their financial security or employment. It shouldn’t take a global pandemic for us to recognize that all workers deserve the freedom and peace of mind that comes with having a paid family and medical leave program. Maryland should declare that it is both good public health policy and good economics to give workers the ability to stay home when they are sick, or care for family members when they need help.
HB386/SB310; HB409/SB403; HB552/SB553: Support our Small Businesses
HB386/SB310
The COVID-19 pandemic illustrated the need for strong community support to keep local businesses and nonprofits afloat in challenging times. This bill would extend the eligible uses of the Small, Minority, and Women-Owned Businesses Account (SMWOBA) to include grants to businesses and nonprofit organizations in areas where a local state of emergency has been declared, providing a needed boost to struggling organizations.
HB409/SB403
This bill eliminates annual filing fees that all business entities are required to pay to maintain their “Good Standing” status with the state that allows them to operate. Maryland currently has one of the highest fees for business filings compared to our neighboring states at $300 for companies, LLCs, and other entities. We are all committed to attracting and retaining businesses to our great state, and the elimination of the filing fee is a small but meaningful way to provide support to Maryland businesses who file electronically
HB552/SB553
Small businesses often operate on small margins, making consistent cash flow critical to their success. Timely payment to small businesses is another crucial aspect of ensuring they can continue to operate effectively. This bill aids in that regard by requiring payment of undisputed retainage upon substantial completion of a project.
These bills represent the promise and possibility of transformative governance. If I’m blessed to be your next Governor, I’ll build on this legislation by implementing my bold platform to lift Marylanders into the high quality of life that they deserve.
Help spread the word about these important bills by sharing with your friends on social media!
Click here to view and reshare on Facebook. [https://www.facebook.com/photo?fbid=516341916524541&set=a.422901372535263]
Click here to view and retweet on Twitter. [https://twitter.com/peterfranchot/status/1494416693438959621/photo/1]
Click here to view and reshare on Instagram. [https://www.instagram.com/p/CaF7tzCBSP_/]
Thank you,
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/81826/images/Franchot%20Signature.png]
By Authority: Franchot Anderson-Walker for Maryland Slate; Thomas C. Brennan, Treasurer.
Franchot Anderson-Walker for Maryland Slate
4831 Tesla Dr, Suite J
Bowie, MD 20715
United States

We're building a grassroots movement -- so make sure to forward this email to a friend!
Want to receive email updates from Team Franchot? Then click here [https://secure.ngpvan.com/MCeSEGOJC0GwhHyQim-AWg2] to sign up!

This bill works to ensure a just transition for employees accustomed to operating and maintaining diesel buses and associated infrastructure. It is crucial to the well-being of MTA employees that they receive the necessary safety and workforce development training to perform their jobs safely and effectively. HB425/SB387: Ban Ghost Guns In Maryland, we lose approximately 500 of our fellow citizens every year to gun-related violence – a toll that falls disproportionately on vulnerable communities and people of color. Despite these alarming statistics, there are still nationwide efforts in both policy, and technology development to make access to firearms easier. The only parties who stand to benefit from the philosophy that ‘more guns mean less crime’ are the firearm manufacturers who have a financial interest in the continued legality of these so-called ‘ghost guns.’ These unserialized firearms are increasingly available, and more frequently used in criminal activity. It is crucial that this bill is enacted into law to help keep these essentially untraceable and uncontrollable weapons off the streets. HB8/SB275: Pass Paid Family and Medical Leave One of the many inequities that the COVID-19 pandemic has revealed is our country’s lack of paid family and medical leave policies. This bill is vitally important to correct that inequity and ensure that workers can care for themselves and loved ones in times of illness without jeopardizing their financial security or employment. It shouldn’t take a global pandemic for us to recognize that all workers deserve the freedom and peace of mind that comes with having a paid family and medical leave program. Maryland should declare that it is both good public health policy and good economics to give workers the ability to stay home when they are sick, or care for family members when they need help. HB386/SB310; HB409/SB403; HB552/SB553: Support our Small Businesses HB386/SB310 The COVID-19 pandemic illustrated the need for strong community support to keep local businesses and nonprofits afloat in challenging times. This bill would extend the eligible uses of the Small, Minority, and Women-Owned Businesses Account (SMWOBA) to include grants to businesses and nonprofit organizations in areas where a local state of emergency has been declared, providing a needed boost to struggling organizations. HB409/SB403 This bill eliminates annual filing fees that all business entities are required to pay to maintain their “Good Standing” status with the state that allows them to operate. Maryland currently has one of the highest fees for business filings compared to our neighboring states at $300 for companies, LLCs, and other entities. We are all committed to attracting and retaining businesses to our great state, and the elimination of the filing fee is a small but meaningful way to provide support to Maryland businesses who file electronically HB552/SB553 Small businesses often operate on small margins, making consistent cash flow critical to their success. Timely payment to small businesses is another crucial aspect of ensuring they can continue to operate effectively. This bill aids in that regard by requiring payment of undisputed retainage upon substantial completion of a project. These bills represent the promise and possibility of transformative governance. If I’m blessed to be your next Governor, I’ll build on this legislation by implementing my bold platform to lift Marylanders into the high quality of life that they deserve. Help spread the word about these important bills by sharing with your friends on social media! Click here to view and reshare on Facebook. [] Click here to view and retweet on Twitter. [] Click here to view and reshare on Instagram. [] Thank you, [] By Authority: Franchot Anderson-Walker for Maryland Slate; Thomas C. Brennan, Treasurer. Franchot Anderson-Walker for Maryland Slate 4831 Tesla Dr, Suite J Bowie, MD 20715 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: . We're building a grassroots movement -- so make sure to forward this email to a friend! Want to receive email updates from Team Franchot? Then click here [] to sign up!

name: Christy Smith
subject: I would not be where I am without community college
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress Christy Smith for Congress
body:
|
---

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41951916/331021819/-1614289773?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZjM3Y2FjZC1kMzkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CABKU2_Tpma9ainpoXWEAlAY5DB3O9SW8TGNAMkHcQY=&emci=ed85312a-5f8f-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&emdi=4f37cacd-d390-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&ceid=11385749)

Dear Peter,

Many of you know I am a proud graduate of California's stellar community college system. Being raised by a single working mother, money for university tuition simply wasn't available to me, so I enrolled at the College of the Canyons – an excellent community college in my hometown of Santa Clarita.

This is the same college my mother attended when she was putting herself through nursing school to support our family. **Community college saved our family** and launched my career in public service; I would not be where I am without it.

**That's why I support Gov. Newsom's efforts to expand funding for California's community colleges like the College of the Canyons. A high-quality education should be available to any and all who want one.**

[**California gets it, it's time for our national leaders to act. Join me and add your name to the petition urging Congress to make a bold investment in community colleges nationwide. >>>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41951927/331021821/-1135774777?ms=em22_220218_newsomCC&refcode=em22_220218_newsomCC&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZjM3Y2FjZC1kMzkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CABKU2_Tpma9ainpoXWEAlAY5DB3O9SW8TGNAMkHcQY=&emci=ed85312a-5f8f-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&emdi=4f37cacd-d390-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&ceid=11385749)**** [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41951928/331021888/-1135774777?ms=em22_220218_newsomCC&refcode=em22_220218_newsomCC&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZjM3Y2FjZC1kMzkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CABKU2_Tpma9ainpoXWEAlAY5DB3O9SW8TGNAMkHcQY=&emci=ed85312a-5f8f-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&emdi=4f37cacd-d390-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&ceid=11385749) While California's colleges are also some of the most affordable, at $138 per 3-credit course, textbooks are often more than the cost of tuition and prohibitive for many students.

No student should ever have to make the decision between buying groceries or buying their educational materials. I applaud Gov. Newsom's 2022-2023 budget that allocates historic funding for our community colleges and reigning in textbook costs so every student has the opportunity to build the career of their dreams.

**It's time for Congress to follow suit.**

[**Add your name alongside mine to urge Congress to follow California's lead and fund community colleges nationwide. Investment in our students is an investment in the nation, add your name and let's build a brighter future for all. >>>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41951930/331021890/-1135774777?ms=em22_220218_newsomCC&refcode=em22_220218_newsomCC&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZjM3Y2FjZC1kMzkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CABKU2_Tpma9ainpoXWEAlAY5DB3O9SW8TGNAMkHcQY=&emci=ed85312a-5f8f-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&emdi=4f37cacd-d390-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&ceid=11385749) [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41951931/331021892/-1135774777?ms=em22_220218_newsomCC&refcode=em22_220218_newsomCC&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZjM3Y2FjZC1kMzkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CABKU2_Tpma9ainpoXWEAlAY5DB3O9SW8TGNAMkHcQY=&emci=ed85312a-5f8f-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&emdi=4f37cacd-d390-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&ceid=11385749) The opportunity to pursue a fulfilling career should not be a privilege reserved only for those who can afford it. Whether you want to become an architect or a graphic designer, a teacher, an electrician, or even run for public office, community college is the place to make it happen – no matter what income bracket you're in.

Thanks for the support,

Christy

College of the Canyons Alum

[**PS- If you're as fired up about making college affordable as I am, consider a $5 donation or more to my campaign. Making higher education accessible to all is a top priority of this campaign, and one I will not stop fighting for as CA-27's next representative. clean_body: | Dear Peter, Many of you know I am a proud graduate of California's stellar community college system. Being raised by a single working mother, money for university tuition simply wasn't available to me, so I enrolled at the College of the Canyons – an excellent community college in my hometown of Santa Clarita. This is the same college my mother attended when she was putting herself through nursing school to support our family. Community college saved our family and launched my career in public service; I would not be where I am without it. That's why I support Gov. Newsom's efforts to expand funding for California's community colleges like the College of the Canyons. A high-quality education should be available to any and all who want one. ** While California's colleges are also some of the most affordable, at $138 per 3-credit course, textbooks are often more than the cost of tuition and prohibitive for many students. No student should ever have to make the decision between buying groceries or buying their educational materials. I applaud Gov. Newsom's 2022-2023 budget that allocates historic funding for our community colleges and reigning in textbook costs so every student has the opportunity to build the career of their dreams. It's time for Congress to follow suit. The opportunity to pursue a fulfilling career should not be a privilege reserved only for those who can afford it. Whether you want to become an architect or a graphic designer, a teacher, an electrician, or even run for public office, community college is the place to make it happen – no matter what income bracket you're in. Thanks for the support, Christy College of the Canyons Alum =

should Democrats legalize cannabis?

|

| | Big news from D.C.

Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer just announced that this Spring, [ Democrats will introduce a bill to reform federal cannabis laws!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41952911/331026570/-451217501?refcode=EM_BS_GEL_220218_FR_CANNABIS_A0_na_U1_X1_M1&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMzY3NGIxNC1kNTkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=00YdhPqNp6jzGw0na5sIMFd04LfRA40E-eLuMcS55bw=)

This is a huge deal. The proposed legislation — known as the Cannabis Administration and Opportunity Act (CAOA) — would remove cannabis from the list of controlled substances, expunge non-violent offenses, and finally give our veterans legal access.

[Eddie knows we need to move past the failed War on Cannabis.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41952912/331026571/-451217501?refcode=EM_BS_GEL_220218_FR_CANNABIS_A0_na_U1_X1_M1&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMzY3NGIxNC1kNTkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=00YdhPqNp6jzGw0na5sIMFd04LfRA40E-eLuMcS55bw=) As of today, 37 states have legalized cannabis for recreational or medicinal purposes. But — our federal laws still treat “weed” the same as they did in the 1980s!

And despite our changing national attitudes, too many folks — disproportionately people of color — are still being persecuted for using cannabis. That’s why, when Eddie is elected, he will work to reform federal law and end the federal prohibition of cannabis!

[If you think it’s high time that the US reformed its cannabis laws, can you chip in $5.00 to Eddie Geller’s GRASSroots campaign to flip FL-15 BLUE?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41952913/331026573/-451217501?refcode=EM_BS_GEL_220218_FR_CANNABIS_A0_na_U1_X1_M1&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMzY3NGIxNC1kNTkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=00YdhPqNp6jzGw0na5sIMFd04LfRA40E-eLuMcS55bw=) | | | [CHIP IN $5 >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41952914/331026600/1783234547?refcode=EM_BS_GEL_220218_FR_CANNABIS_A0_na_U1_X1_M1&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMzY3NGIxNC1kNTkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=00YdhPqNp6jzGw0na5sIMFd04LfRA40E-eLuMcS55bw=)

name: MadisonHorn.com
subject: Madison told us to reach out
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Oklahomans for Madison
body:
| Look: Unlike Lankford, Madison is not a career politician. She doesn’t want to do only what’s convenient and ignore what Oklahomans actually need.

Lankford has consistently voted against things that will help us: affordable healthcare access, reproductive rights, expanding rural broadband, equity for LGBTQ+ Oklahomans, and more.

_He votes only for what his special interest backers and party leadership want him to._

**Madison will fight like hell for all Oklahomans—rural and urban—because, coming from rural Stilwell and living in Oklahoma City now, she gets it.**

**[But, electing our first woman Senator isn’t going to be easy. We need to raise another $5,000 on digital this month to keep building our framework. clean_body: | | | We have breaking news, Peter: Alek Skarlatos has officially filed to run for Congress again. You remember Skarlatos, right? He's the right-wing pro-Trump extremist that spent over $5 million blasting Congressman DeFazio with scurrilous attack ads. Now he's back and he's running the same playbook. Already, House Republican leader Kevin McCarthy is backing Skarlatos, and his campaign has raised over $1 million in 2021. McCarthy and Skarlatos think the GOP takeover of Congress starts in Oregon. Kevin McCarthy even said so himself when he said, "We've proven that [Republicans] can win from Miami to Minnesota to California and I think you're going to see things in Oregon." = We're going to win the same way DeFazio won – with a tremendous showing of grassroots support. = Our movement is growing each and every day. Peter,

Recently, I was asked on Fox News, after all the tough fights I’ve had, why I’m getting back up to run again.

_**This was my answer ⬇**_
[](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=55e5538cf6024fc5aadcae542bf775ac&_e=xAuth1JAepSQdMeeKsQfWoU4akQKzVNrNNrtdvFpkVf9h66jP6-M_KDwnsu3XeToaOoTsATCpA7yv4u4VuGCdJUZhcGwpe1Ag18LSXPiJDeD1IdmbVANpDpp6JE7CHLl2B3EXroFYqf7igfR-QUkbkqvVq22PxxyatbajhXZRgpaBEX-rQ2FhJgdPZMapyp7MQZydW2eFwvgRXI-hd-Gi71z-PIIqC6ThwIc_EseVhASmvZQlJ5wALhIwlt73hQdfIp2X1fdXUyRioSBYynPUq5hJH_bun-6twxVO5IPxfJ9M4tXAOKs-uol9DGthtVxc4J5_ajMSvOjRxo7B4TWmOYI4x_W2OatFxxcQ6kpjR4InSkzU_BZ4XUsEhC_j-GbQyftOfhx_Si3pb22FWiPxtc31Ara7BLBflnprdV_n9jjPYdimdQhwcGw8wv4XJknjultmjgl3NseZu3O2WABCel8WZTFinn6hLft1ZBG3f8%3D)
Peter, Americans are fed up...

_**Fed up with gas prices.**_

Fed up with the price of food going up.

_**Fed up with having their concerns put second, not first.**_

Fed up with having no choice of what and how and when and where their kids are going to be educated.

_**Fed up with having their freedom taken away from them.**_

**Freedom is on the ballot right now, and our future is on the ballot right now.**

I believe the people of Michigan and America are going to stand up and take their future back.

We’re going to take our freedom back and demand accountability. We’re going to take the house majority back, and it'll be an honor to be a part of that class to flip the house.

**Now let’s get his country back to sanityー****fuel of our fight today >>**

Thank you,
John James
_Get to know John:_
-At age 17, he became determined to serve his country.
-He graduated from West Point, then served as a pilot in Operation Iraqi Freedom, leading two Apache platoons.
-After 8 years of service, he returned home to Michigan to work in the family business.
-He runs a family business, James Group International, an automotive supply chain and logistics company.
-Now, John lives with his wife Elizabeth and their three young sons in Michigan.

body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41954876/331036093/2140832878?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNTQyOTQzMS1kYzkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ssnfrD1_h6-fnEu4zGR8bBlkzQGTbI3hTEaXUG4Z2xc=&emci=877d6199-2e90-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&emdi=25429431-dc90-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&ceid=9818364)
---

| | It's tax season. Millions of working families are getting ready to file their taxes for 2021, while **far too many are still waiting on their tax returns from 2020.** Thanks to decades of Republican budget cuts, the IRS is understaffed, underfunded, and without the resources it needs to serve the American people. Meanwhile, as working people pay their taxes, tax cheats are getting away without paying their fair share, knowing that their chances of getting audited are shrinking every single day. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41954877/331036094/-2044921950?refcode=2022-02-18-taxseason&recurring=0&amount=17&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=25429431-dc90-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNTQyOTQzMS1kYzkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ssnfrD1_h6-fnEu4zGR8bBlkzQGTbI3hTEaXUG4Z2xc=) Let's be clear: this is the direct result of the decades-long war that Republicans have waged on the IRS. Protecting tax cheats while working families are expected to pay taxes out of each paycheck is what Republicans have been working to solidify for years. And they've succeeded. I'm using every tool at my disposal to ensure the IRS gets the full funding it needs to go after tax cheats and serve the American people and ensure once and for all that everyone pays their fair share. **This should be an easy bipartisan victory for fairness. But it has become another front in the right-wing war on working people.** I'm fighting with everything I've got to get this done. _Ron_ Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | clean_body: | | | It's tax season. Millions of working families are getting ready to file their taxes for 2021, while far too many are still waiting on their tax returns from 2020. Thanks to decades of Republican budget cuts, the IRS is understaffed, underfunded, and without the resources it needs to serve the American people. Meanwhile, as working people pay their taxes, tax cheats are getting away without paying their fair share, knowing that their chances of getting audited are shrinking every single day. = Let's be clear: this is the direct result of the decades-long war that Republicans have waged on the IRS. Protecting tax cheats while working families are expected to pay taxes out of each paycheck is what Republicans have been working to solidify for years. And they've succeeded. I'm using every tool at my disposal to ensure the IRS gets the full funding it needs to go after tax cheats and serve the American people and ensure once and for all that everyone pays their fair share. This should be an easy bipartisan victory for fairness. But it has become another front in the right-wing war on working people. I'm fighting with everything I've got to get this done. body:
Indiana Senate GOP has finally admitted that they’ve crossed the line

**Peter — the Indiana Republican Party has become the Culture War Party.** They prefer pursuing divisive culture wars instead of delivering on those kitchen-table issues that matter most to Hoosier families. Meanwhile, Indiana Democrats have their nose to the grindstone working hard to continue to deliver a brighter economic future for folks across Indiana. **Hoosier families have a better future overall because of President Biden, Indiana Democrats, and the Jobs Act.** **[Can you make a contribution to the Indiana Democratic Party so that we can continue to deliver for Hoosiers?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41955593/331039097/405560545?refcode=idp-em-220218-culture-war-party&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZmY0ODAwYS1kZDkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hcjO1IdhV55e581RSFyG9jOVcPJJrhqvvIZl8_TVkWY=)** Politicizing public education, discrediting the COVID-19 vaccine, and restricting voting rights for Hoosiers — **Republican partisanship already succeeded at dividing communities across Indiana.** **Indiana Senate Republicans have _finally_ admitted that their culture wars have crossed the line.** They’ve drastically watered down the GOP’s 2022 national agenda to make it more palatable instead of focusing on kitchen-table issues like new jobs, repaired roads and bridges, broadband, and regional economic development projects in every corner of the state. **President Biden, Indiana Democrats, and the Jobs Act have made good on promises of a brighter economic future for Hoosiers. body:
| | |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41956340/331042240/1254848285?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODk0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYmE3ZWU3ZS1kZTkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Urwal8lfjO6HWMi6E1oKEBG-3rCEh7mCuY4vEPsCIyA=&emci=d2a1ad64-3a90-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&emdi=3ba7ee7e-de90-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&ceid=23594672)

| | |
---
Peter, it’s Shannon – Holly’s new campaign manager. I’m an Alabama native and a proud alumna of Mississippi State University. I moved to DC to start my Masters in Political Communication and to work on Capitol Hill before moving back home to the south. After long days of working hard to unseat Marjorie Taylor Greene, I spend my time exploring NW Georgia’s pristine landscapes with my dog Bing. Bing serves as Holly’s field director and the Head of Treats.
---
---
I wanted to share with y’all some of the amazing accomplishments we’ve achieved so far, and our next steps. **WHAT TEAM HOLLY HAS ACCOMPLISHED**
* We’ve raised over $1.5 million from over 50,000 donors all across the country.
* We’ve sent out 7,000 voter registration packets.
* We got kicked out of Marge’s town hall.
* We’ve talked to thousands of voters and made them feel heard.
* We’ve amassed over 185,000 thousand followers on Twitter.

**OUR NEXT STEPS**
* We’re planning to send out the rest of our voter registration packets.
* We’re mobilizing our supporters and putting on weekly phonebanks.
* We’re attending every county Dem meeting.
* We’re sending out mailers to our constituents.
* We’re texting, calling, and canvassing to make sure our message is heard.
* **[We’re calling on our supporters like you, to help us fund this historic work.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41956341/331042241/-804235033?refcode=em0218&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODk0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYmE3ZWU3ZS1kZTkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Urwal8lfjO6HWMi6E1oKEBG-3rCEh7mCuY4vEPsCIyA=) **

We are going to win this race, but only if we do it together. I’m so proud to be working for the people of Northwest Georgia and I know that we WILL unseat Marjorie Taylor Greene in November. **[Can you rush $14 today and help us fund our next big steps?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41956342/331042242/-804235033?refcode=em0218&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODk0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYmE3ZWU3ZS1kZTkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Urwal8lfjO6HWMi6E1oKEBG-3rCEh7mCuY4vEPsCIyA=) ** Talk soon!Shannon.

| Peter, **Since the pandemic began, women have left the workforce at a greater rate than men of the same education level, and in the current economic upswing, women are not making the same advances. However, with your support, I know that we can change this and make a difference in the lives of so many women.** While our state is starting to come back economically, we are not seeing the same gains for women. It’s time to ensure that everyone is able to benefit from a surging economy — and we have a plan for it. We created the King-Siri Agenda for Building Women’s Economic Equity to push for equal opportunity, access, and more. _**[If you share our vision, could you help us make it a reality?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41956412/331042457/1974957641?source=email-ngp-20220218-b1_fr_full-MS_S2_C1&&&&refcode=email-ngp-20220218-b1_fr_full-MS_S2_C1&&&&utm_medium=email&&&&utm_source=ngp&&&&utm_campaign=20220218&&&&utm_content=b1_fr_full&&&&utm_term=MS_S2_C1&&&&amount=25&refcode2=1501a3fb-de90-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNTAxYTNmYi1kZTkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gKdYTP44acQInEeu7WxlDmhkQC5bnIsIBvQ1gpLQYCE=)**_ John and I are dedicated to ending the leaky pipeline and building economic opportunities for women by implementing paid family leave, access to affordable childcare, legal protections against sexual harassment, closing the wage gap, and more. Since women are most likely to take on caregiving responsibilities, the lack of paid family leave and childcare disproportionately affects their careers.
I know how difficult it can be to make ends meet while balancing work and family. When I had my first son, I was taken off the partnership track at my law firm and made to feel I couldn’t have both a career and a family. In my work at The Women’s Law Center of Maryland, I have spent the last few years trying to make sure disparities like this do not affect the women of our state — and in the King-Siri Administration, we will do that work on a larger scale.
_**[Will you show your support for our agenda by giving what you can today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41956415/331042460/1974957641?source=email-ngp-20220218-b1_fr_full-MS_S2_C1&&&&refcode=email-ngp-20220218-b1_fr_full-MS_S2_C1&&&&utm_medium=email&&&&utm_source=ngp&&&&utm_campaign=20220218&&&&utm_content=b1_fr_full&&&&utm_term=MS_S2_C1&&&&amount=25&refcode2=1501a3fb-de90-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNTAxYTNmYi1kZTkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gKdYTP44acQInEeu7WxlDmhkQC5bnIsIBvQ1gpLQYCE=)**_

Michelle Siri

Hey Team Crist,

On Wednesday, Charlie spent the day in Jacksonville helping to get out the vote for Tracye Polson for City Council, and now we NEED YOUR HELP!

**[Can you help ensure we keep this seat blue in 2022?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41956731/331043639/1827102043?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTYxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZDRlMzg2MS1lMDkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xreQw2MH6MyU_V2ZQ32sEQhJwy6PpdbamkcpM6XISeg=&emci=72743a49-cf90-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&emdi=4d4e3861-e090-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&ceid=8807089)** Tracye’s election is the first special election in the on year, and Democrats need to show we can turnout BIG in Duval!

Dr. Polson is the fighter and leader we need in Duval County — and Charlie has given her his full support and endorsement for Jacksonville City Council! Tracye knows how to bring people together to stop corruption and create a just and equitable city. Her many years of experience as a small business owner and mental health professional have prepared her to be a determined advocate and leader for all of Jacksonville’s neighborhoods and families.

name: Sen. Tom Cotton
subject: Quintessentially American
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rod Dorilas for Congress

[](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=715a28bef7604e1c8053a36ea2d32513&_e=MNuT8BsA39VCpVLu-LM4qDJfalKMLyuq0cBXrW-qTYvKcyt1iexkSFrw3Q6Y0Dvs5DidB-9ozyvU6pnFhQXeU7RSl-drlpzDm6IfD2Ypzs90QvOFQcVyBVAGnZRqYilgVkdsBek1Lhy_UdB6mO85MsTkNxTDjML8Y03DPSsiUGQ2Xslq0OuLgf3tCs3MdBDQaSBlHRu0hqGLBYNfd_NODVomaIp6Qpk5I3qp_XIGNaTbVV2JYBtgvF3jFp8RfAJ-r6pq1OHq_BLohyhQcH8VbQxwe4yEJZtech1YZZUTrEXJwHs-YdhCpdzwy8G66hOcZC_Xb9nvLDjl_M1iyRO3SbXKxn8JmmuT_sIWgHImRlW7EJSzJCytUEDYHKwIhkT4utsnRqTYi3GiYG6T7wdQyFIZLYX4XK3IY1TolSyCLjev4dbS0RED1DOMK7GR7mV1DJf786Cj-8Xmgus2FKj6WA%3D%3D)

---

Peter,

While I’m not myself up for reelection for a number of years, I’m absolutely dedicated to helping Republicans win back both the Senate majority and the majority in the U.S. House.

When I come across promising candidates for office, I make a concerted effort to do everything I personally can to help them succeed. That’s why today I’m excited to endorse Mr. Rod Dorilás for Congress.

**Rod’s Dorilás’ story is quintessentially American.** His Haitian immigrant parents came to the United States without a penny to their name; to make a living they picked crops in South Florida. With hard work and a lot of perseverance, they found their own American Dream and became small business owners. And less than forty years after first arriving in the United States, their son – Rod – had the distinct honor of serving the president of the United States.

I’m proud to support the son of entrepreneurial immigrants who built their own American Dream without the help of the government, or socialist-style handouts. **Perhaps with more dedicated public servants such as Rod Dorilás in Congress, grit, determination and personal responsibility may finally win out over the left’s mantra of “give me give me give me.”**

I know it’s a lot to ask, particularly with inflation so high at the moment, **[but if there were ever a candidate worth of a $2 or $3 donation, Rod would be that candidate.](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=715a28bef7604e1c8053a36ea2d32513&_e=MNuT8BsA39VCpVLu-LM4qDJfalKMLyuq0cBXrW-qTYvKcyt1iexkSFrw3Q6Y0Dvs5DidB-9ozyvU6pnFhQXeU7RSl-drlpzDm6IfD2Ypzs90QvOFQcVyBVAGnZRqYilgVkdsBek1Lhy_UdB6mO85MsTkNxTDjML8Y03DPSsiUGQ2Xslq0OuLgf3tCs3MdBDQaSBlHRu0hqGLBYNfd_NODVomaIp6Qpk5I3qp_XIGNaTbVV2JYBtgvF3jFp8RfAJ-4NYVjKd7cYGs5xUFD8cJrWkXIcK2_P00MrrV37whMoDK-CqyH-JAX8VtsFRbHzGs_S0oaDngmFIMXeT1Al7BFzsvxzpdbrsQVDWfvfurARM-iVKvpiOOBats156zCVwoNi_vYbqxLBO1hv_3MFqZKwhJeOCmN-BenixNWy5LaTKbfHPczoQPNRvEOYdhji51fGldc6__aFVYGHfe8raIpw%3D%3D)**

[**Send an American Patriot to Congress
Chip in $2>> **](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=715a28bef7604e1c8053a36ea2d32513&_e=MNuT8BsA39VCpVLu-LM4qDJfalKMLyuq0cBXrW-qTYvKcyt1iexkSFrw3Q6Y0Dvs5DidB-9ozyvU6pnFhQXeU7RSl-drlpzDm6IfD2Ypzs90QvOFQcVyBVAGnZRqYilgVkdsBek1Lhy_UdB6mO85MsTkNxTDjML8Y03DPSsiUGQ2Xslq0OuLgf3tCs3MdBDQaSBlHRu0hqGLBYNfd_NODVomaIp6Qpk5I3qp_XIGNaTbVV2JYBtgvF3jFp8RfAJ-bcxfga9QQ5Qm5VfDCj6PxYXDlewu_qbpNA9euZ5ZzQ_sdfBsDL4atSZAumZk1c1XzisxEdpNhjbsrm7nNQZU9y5EiIKtRQNDKrfX7mTGEFMbTwik6Xnk-yNboWZuHBirhMZlNl4y74XugzQvnoW9TQRFkL0pqT1ZLQZ8tc3QUexsXCeyR6389cQypaLPx-8yEWP6WMvbRxetyr0cRBcgJQ%3D%3D)
Rod is exactly the kind of representative that the American people need. As a veteran, first generation American, small business owner, and former counsel in the Department of Commerce, Rod brings the experience and energy to make real change in Washington D.C.

Rod is a fighter and the Republican candidate who can win in the South Florida district where he’s running. **[Rod Dorilás has my full backing and I’m asking you to join me in supporting his campaign today.](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=715a28bef7604e1c8053a36ea2d32513&_e=MNuT8BsA39VCpVLu-LM4qDJfalKMLyuq0cBXrW-qTYvKcyt1iexkSFrw3Q6Y0Dvs5DidB-9ozyvU6pnFhQXeU7RSl-drlpzDm6IfD2Ypzs90QvOFQcVyBVAGnZRqYilgVkdsBek1Lhy_UdB6mO85MsTkNxTDjML8Y03DPSsiUGQ2Xslq0OuLgf3tCs3MdBDQaSBlHRu0hqGLBYNfd_NODVomaIp6Qpk5I3qp_XIGNaTbVV2JYBtgvF3jFp8RfAJ-Yo8TZIEH9atZJ177i2viZu1B7qcKZc6m50RwB0raC7rNZ69cFCaRO7_F7BkbWatmejZSEVEnLSc-o_MWIbloKBWH-L7LFXqAd8O3ssBepGFsDSKUn_yTGmaEO2sU9GDRp_LjJEKyOoVQS3BrG7O5E3e8jYZCzroKj4NTYtKJmV88hQplt-kEnA9ruI7j4ryPZxkgl0EagwTvyQQLivAmTw%3D%3D)**

[**Send an American Patriot to Congress
Chip in $2>> **](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=715a28bef7604e1c8053a36ea2d32513&_e=MNuT8BsA39VCpVLu-LM4qDJfalKMLyuq0cBXrW-qTYvKcyt1iexkSFrw3Q6Y0Dvs5DidB-9ozyvU6pnFhQXeU7RSl-drlpzDm6IfD2Ypzs90QvOFQcVyBVAGnZRqYilgVkdsBek1Lhy_UdB6mO85MsTkNxTDjML8Y03DPSsiUGQ2Xslq0OuLgf3tCs3MdBDQaSBlHRu0hqGLBYNfd_NODVomaIp6Qpk5I3qp_XIGNaTbVV2JYBtgvF3jFp8RfAJ-gZ0Aw5lcV1RlovvHtLzh5oBqFLTOLZeDO-FK1i5GzQFXKEZjp4XC1Wc4uko6fafoXSulsqPyu26KZ9FWrEGEqIvCFUk6ddwGIPjXA1QZ3qXrc_F4tx_1wXm-Y9ss4fbXWoDTr8PUTbcYEdYqea8bN56eG1-tvj-fi7GaBoPwOjvXx2xV_Fv0KVxRrFU8mtGakcrSnSLS28Fcwm7HJ9xtpQ%3D%3D)
In solidarity,
Sen. Tom Cotton

| _**ABOUT ROD**_

Rod Dorilás is the first-generation son of two immigrants from Haiti. His father immigrated to Florida to pick crops, and eventually his parents went on to own a small business. From a young age, his parents instilled in him the importance of hard work, integrity, and service.

At age 17, Rod enlisted in the United States Navy, serving for six years as a Petty Officer Second Class and Assistant Engagement Control Officer onboard the USS The Sullivans, stationed in Jacksonville. There, he was responsible for planning and executing the deployment of Tomahawk Land Attack Missiles on the Ballistic Missile Defense destroyer.

Upon completion of his military service, Rod attended law school at Syracuse University with the help of the G.I. Bill. After graduating, he served in President Trump's Administration in the U.S. Department of Commerce. While serving our great nation, Rod fought tirelessly to ensure that American families and businesses weren't taken advantage of by China.

Rod is a true public servant and is dedicated to serving his country and community. He wants to ensure that every American, through hard work and perseverance, can achieve the American Dream. Rod is running for Congress in South Florida to continue Trump's America First Agenda and defend our country from the Radical Left.

[**Will you stand with Rod?**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=715a28bef7604e1c8053a36ea2d32513&_e=MNuT8BsA39VCpVLu-LM4qDJfalKMLyuq0cBXrW-qTYvKcyt1iexkSFrw3Q6Y0Dvs5DidB-9ozyvU6pnFhQXeU7RSl-drlpzDm6IfD2Ypzs90QvOFQcVyBVAGnZRqYilgVkdsBek1Lhy_UdB6mO85MsTkNxTDjML8Y03DPSsiUGQ2Xslq0OuLgf3tCs3MdBDQaSBlHRu0hqGLBYNfd_NODVomaIp6Qpk5I3qp_XIGNaTbVV2JYBtgvF3jFp8RfAJ-j5xqdwR0gKpm-ZFSu2RyVY4rJbWItFk3OKwPMpSc073aD0Pr6ESPc-z84sKFUb5V0Ko2wzMtZ0lx1BDdHNck_q-IMZIs_OdlmSzQnHwPPMoproELaMxRd3MGkQ8jllPLoZkT4ECNItWfmouE-BpEVXnKzQt-PFUy3LzUiamAuDhtoRZu5W6n69WLmb9qsumYiAK0YNkxrTtOnW_bzVN7mQ%3D%3D)

**House Republican Conference Chair Elise Stefanik endorses Harriet Hageman**
---
Rep. Elise Stefanik, chair of the House Republican Conference, today endorsed Harriet Hageman in her campaign against incumbent Rep. Liz Cheney in Wyoming. Stefanik was elected chair of the House Republican Conference in May 2021 after Cheney was removed from the position. Hageman has also been endorsed by President Donald J. Trump, Republican Leader Kevin McCarthy, and Sen. Rand Paul of Kentucky. Stefanik today issued the following statement: _“I’m proud to endorse Harriet Hageman in her race to unseat Liz Cheney. House Republicans were ready for a change when I took over as Conference Chair, and it’s resoundingly clear that Wyoming families are too. _ _ _ _“Liz Cheney abandoned her constituents to become a Far-Left Pelosi puppet. Liz sadly belongs in an MSNBC or CNN news chair, not in Congress representing Wyoming—a state that voted for President Trump by over forty points. _ _ _ _“Harriet is a true America First patriot who will restore the people of Wyoming’s voice that has been long forgotten by Liz Cheney.” _ Hageman issued the following statement: _“I am honored and grateful to have the support of Rep. Elise Stefanik, who is an excellent representative for her own constituents and a fantastic chairwoman of the House Republican Conference. She is proof that it is possible to fight for your principles in Washington, D.C. while also staying true to what your constituents want you to do. _ _ _ _“It is fitting that I am receiving the endorsement of the congresswoman who replaced Liz Cheney as chair of the Republican Conference, because it exemplifies exactly how Cheney has abandoned her responsibilities. In Wyoming, we only get one member of the House of Representatives, and so we have to get it right. Cheney has turned her back on us, and used the Congressional seat we entrusted her with to further her own personal goals. At the same time, she has aided Speaker Nancy Pelosi in damaging the Republican Party as a willing pawn of the Democrats. When I am the Congresswoman from Wyoming, I will always remember who sent me to Congress, and I will always fight for what is best for our wonderful state.” _ ###

clean_body: | | | | House Republican Conference Chair Elise Stefanik endorses Harriet Hageman Rep. Elise Stefanik, chair of the House Republican Conference, today endorsed Harriet Hageman in her campaign against incumbent Rep. Liz Cheney in Wyoming. Stefanik was elected chair of the House Republican Conference in May 2021 after Cheney was removed from the position. Hageman has also been endorsed by President Donald J. Trump, Republican Leader Kevin McCarthy, and Sen. Rand Paul of Kentucky. Stefanik today issued the following statement: “I’m proud to endorse Harriet Hageman in her race to unseat Liz Cheney. House Republicans were ready for a change when I took over as Conference Chair, and it’s resoundingly clear that Wyoming families are too. “Liz Cheney abandoned her constituents to become a Far-Left Pelosi puppet. Liz sadly belongs in an MSNBC or CNN news chair, not in Congress representing Wyoming—a state that voted for President Trump by over forty points. “Harriet is a true America First patriot who will restore the people of Wyoming’s voice that has been long forgotten by Liz Cheney.” Hageman issued the following statement: “I am honored and grateful to have the support of Rep. Elise Stefanik, who is an excellent representative for her own constituents and a fantastic chairwoman of the House Republican Conference. She is proof that it is possible to fight for your principles in Washington, D.C. while also staying true to what your constituents want you to do. “It is fitting that I am receiving the endorsement of the congresswoman who replaced Liz Cheney as chair of the Republican Conference, because it exemplifies exactly how Cheney has abandoned her responsibilities. In Wyoming, we only get one member of the House of Representatives, and so we have to get it right. Cheney has turned her back on us, and used the Congressional seat we entrusted her with to further her own personal goals. At the same time, she has aided Speaker Nancy Pelosi in damaging the Republican Party as a willing pawn of the Democrats. When I am the Congresswoman from Wyoming, I will always remember who sent me to Congress, and I will always fight for what is best for our wonderful state.” ### | | | |

name: Senator John Kennedy
subject: I don’t know how to say this.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by John Kennedy for Us
body:
[View this message in your browser](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z7hmo5mai89oajjvtq3ou4rhmsek9tuga8jbu69g_rp22sh2s8i66ob5c9g6eoj2cth238h5e8he485he5o75pt7kkpq8ddl69qbcd1jm4qj6d9jmpqj7d9neppqcdtlmui34thgmipnadt4sbp65slmmhob1pb3e2oe6sphe5qmaobj6kq6at3jc8ob0ojm6ipn6spk65o68ob3cqrrfkjge9i3cc5icpp78cbmc3gb8p9gs2qj2p76cpj3ap5lm5jrcpr66jgb6p9nm2qmash5u)
It’s worse than I thought.

| [](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2zs2bpitqkb9uq87pa6m4bmgb1ovj2g2sr6hk0ah0_rp22sh2s8i66ob5c9g6eoj2cth2bs)
---

Friend,

It’s not the easiest thing to admit, but I’m getting beat like a rented mule in fundraising by my new Radical Opponent. It’s not a good sign for me, you, or any other conservative in Louisiana as we start 2022.

I’ll be blunt: If I don’t catch up to him and catch up quick, he’s going to blow me out of the water come November and threaten to turn Louisiana and the U.S. Senate into a Socialist safe haven. I won’t have it, and I hope you won’t either, but I’m coming to you in all sincerity when I say I can’t do this by myself.

I’ve got to have your help if I’m going to have a chance. I’ve got to have your help if we’re going to save America. It’s not up for debate. Your support is the answer. Your support is the only thing standing in the way of Nancy Pelosi’s handpicked dream candidate wiping the floor with me this coming November. I’m humbly asking for your help. **Without it, they’ll keep burying me with their unlimited dollars.

I’M ASKING FOR YOUR HELP.

WITHOUT IT I CAN’T STOP THE LEFT. **

God Bless you and your generosity,

name: Dick Durbin
subject: We have two choices
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin
body:
I could use more allies in the Senate fighting alongside me.
#
Peter,
We have two choices this November: We can elect more Democratic allies to the Senate who will act on climate change, comprehensive immigration reform, prescription drug prices, and more.
Or we can hand over the Senate to Mitch McConnell and face two years of guaranteed obstruction on all our progress.
**[We cannot allow McConnell and the GOP to take back the Senate. We’ve set a 48-hour $5,000 goal to stay on track with our plans to support Democratic Senate candidates this month. Can you chip in $5 now?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41959070/331052853/-777994057?refcode=dd_em_220218_1_a&amount=5&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODQ4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MDY2ZWNmYi1lNjkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QIbr8oGT__gthnOcz3xKayPL-gHDlLwBEEbEbpUMKno=)**
I’m proud of the work we’ve done this past year to confirm qualified judges, invest in our infrastructure, and support families and small businesses through the pandemic.
But we can’t stop there. I’m pushing to pass legislation that would extend the child tax credit, lower drug prices, invest in reforming our immigration system, and tackle other pressing challenges we face – and I could use more allies in the Senate fighting alongside me.
**[Will you chip in $5 now to support Democratic Senate candidates who will join me in making progress for working families?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41959071/331052854/-777994057?refcode=dd_em_220218_1_a&amount=5&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODQ4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MDY2ZWNmYi1lNjkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QIbr8oGT__gthnOcz3xKayPL-gHDlLwBEEbEbpUMKno=)**
My best,
Dick Durbin

name: Greg Casar
subject: Bernie, AOC, Warren, and YOU
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greg Casar for Congress
body:
This is it, Peter.
Election Day is 11 days away and it’s all-hands-on-deck to Get Out The Vote. That’s why in the last few weeks you’ve heard from Bernie Sanders, Elizabeth Warren, and AOC —all asking you to help bring our campaign to victory.
These progressive leaders know how important this race is—and I know you do too. Can you join Bernie Sanders, Elizabeth Warren, and AOC in supporting our campaign in this final stretch? With their help and yours, we can win this thing on March 1st. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/greg-casar-for-congress?refcode=02.18_email]
JOIN BERNIE, WARREN, & AOC >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/greg-casar-for-congress?refcode=02.18_email]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/greg-casar-for-congress?refcode=02.18_email [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/greg-casar-for-congress?refcode=02.18_email]
I’m so proud of the campaign we’ve run together, Peter. With your help, we’ve built a movement of hardworking Texans who want good jobs, Medicare for All, reproductive rights, and a functional power grid. These are the things I’ll fight for and deliver when I’m elected.
But this campaign isn’t just about me. It’s about a better future for working families and everyday Texans who have been left behind by elected leaders who only serve the wealthy and powerful.
Bernie Sanders, Elizabeth Warren, and AOC have joined our movement because they’re ready to fight for that future. Can I count on you to join us? Pitch in right now to help us Get Out The Vote in this final stretch. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/greg-casar-for-congress?refcode=02.18_email]
Thank you,
Greg
ELECTION DAY IS 11 DAYS AWAY>>DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/greg-casar-for-congress?refcode=02.18_email]
http://facebook.com/gregcasar [http://facebook.com/gregcasar] http://twitter.com/gregcasar [http://twitter.com/gregcasar] http://instagram.com/gregcasar [http://instagram.com/gregcasar]

With their help and yours, we can win this thing on March 1st. [] JOIN BERNIE, WARREN, & AOC >> [] [] I’m so proud of the campaign we’ve run together, Peter. With your help, we’ve built a movement of hardworking Texans who want good jobs, Medicare for All, reproductive rights, and a functional power grid. These are the things I’ll fight for and deliver when I’m elected. But this campaign isn’t just about me. It’s about a better future for working families and everyday Texans who have been left behind by elected leaders who only serve the wealthy and powerful. Bernie Sanders, Elizabeth Warren, and AOC have joined our movement because they’re ready to fight for that future. Can I count on you to join us? Pitch in right now to help us Get Out The Vote in this final stretch. [] Thank you, Greg ELECTION DAY IS 11 DAYS AWAY>>DONATE [] [] [] [] Paid for by Greg Casar for Congress Greg Casar for Congress P.O. Box 301923 Austin, TX 78703 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

body:
We’ll never let this go. | | **URGENT:**

Today we want to remind our supporters that it’s been over 13 months since Republican Reps. Lauren Boebert, Sens. Josh Hawley, Ted Cruz, John Kennedy, and other GOP extremists helped Donald Trump incite the deadly Capitol insurrection —then voted to overturn the 2020 election. **Yet, NOT A SINGLE ONE of these far-right seditionists in Congress has been held accountable.**

Friend,
We don’t care **_how_** hard Republicans like Donald Trump and the right-wing media fight to rewrite what happened on January 6th.
We don’t care **_how_** long it’s been since GOP lawmakers broke their oath when they voted to overturn a free and fair election just hours after an attempted coup.
We don’t care **_how_** many times Republican leaders call on Democrats to move on from one of the darkest days of our democracy.
**Here’s why we’ll never stop demanding accountability — actions have consequences. Especially treasonous acts that result in American deaths and a disgraced former president whose followers continue to make violent threats.**
We can either stand up and hold these GOP liars and traitors accountable. Or we can let them further undermine our democracy with conspiracy theories and violence.
The choice is ours, friend. We know what we have to do.

body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41959689/331055622/-272948131?refcode=AU_MTH_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20220218_PM1_V2_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkM2VmYTE5ZS1lOTkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bH_FDfCDOM9V3Ho1IVDhkj-jNcKTnYLFX-DA2o6TZag=&emci=735a5d98-e990-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&emdi=d3efa19e-e990-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&ceid=252045)

Team, methane emissions are one of the biggest drivers of the climate crisis. That’s why Martin has worked so hard to advance legislation to cut these harmful emissions.
* He’s led the charge to pass bipartisan legislation to cut methane emissions, an effort President Biden signed into law last year. **It was the most significant action Congress has taken on climate in over a decade.**

* And he attended last year’s COP26 global climate summit to address the climate crisis and continue to tackle the hard work ahead. **This was when the U.S. announced big plans to combat climate change by cutting methane emissions in oil and gas operations.**

It will truly take all of us coming together to make sure we cut methane emissions and address the climate emergency with the urgency it requires. That’s why we’re asking you today: **Can you show your support for bold climate action by chipping in any amount? Your donations are critical to helping Martin stay in the U.S. Senate so he can keep up his work to protect our planet.** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41959690/331055623/-317129921?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=AU_MTH_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20220218_PM1_V2_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkM2VmYTE5ZS1lOTkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bH_FDfCDOM9V3Ho1IVDhkj-jNcKTnYLFX-DA2o6TZag=)

clean_body: Team, methane emissions are one of the biggest drivers of the climate crisis. That’s why Martin has worked so hard to advance legislation to cut these harmful emissions. He’s led the charge to pass bipartisan legislation to cut methane emissions, an effort President Biden signed into law last year. It was the most significant action Congress has taken on climate in over a decade. And he attended last year’s COP26 global climate summit to address the climate crisis and continue to tackle the hard work ahead. This was when the U.S. announced big plans to combat climate change by cutting methane emissions in oil and gas operations. It will truly take all of us coming together to make sure we cut methane emissions and address the climate emergency with the urgency it requires. That’s why we’re asking you today: Can you show your support for bold climate action by chipping in any amount? Your donations are critical to helping Martin stay in the U.S. Senate so he can keep up his work to protect our planet. name: Toby Anderson
subject: National Caregiver’s Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Toby Anderson for Congress Inc
body:
| | | [](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/5ca70e78c0100c23041ad8cde91a9694/38863/141ddfa5a71f2d30/2a3066b406edd0cbc3d6b838967d376c/38863)
---
Peter, Today is National Caregiver’s Day, and I just wanted to send a note to all those who have dedicated themselves to caring for others. This day is set aside each year for those who provide personal care, and physical and emotional support to those who need it most.
---
[](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/5ca70e78c0100c23041ad8cde91a9694/38863/141ddfa5a71f2d30/c15f8c311d424219c3d6b838967d376c/38863)
---
As a small business owner in the health care industry and a caregiver myself, I know the sacrifices made by caregivers every single day. While some provide care in a professional setting, others do so in an unpaid capacity, as they volunteer their time and effort for family members or other loved ones. All our caregivers deserve recognition and gratitude for their commitment to selflessly proving care. Thanks for all you do,
Toby Anderson
---
id:2022-02-16-22:47:00:628t
---

name: Delina DiSanto
subject: A deep dive into Schweikert's FEC fine >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Delina DiSanto for Congress
body:
https://www.delinadisanto.com/ [https://www.delinadisanto.com/]
As you know, David Schweikert’s campaign recently agreed to pay a $125,000 fine to the Federal Election Commission for violating campaign finance law. What you might not realize, however, is that he can use campaign donations to pay the fine.
If David Schweikert won't even respect his own donors , you can bet that working Arizonans aren't faring much better...
Can you help me REMOVE David Schweikert from Congress by chipping in anything you can right now? It’s time Arizonans have a trusted leader who will find solutions to the problems facing working families >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/delina-disanto-for-congress-1?refcode=X_021822_EthicsFollowUp]
Schweikert is trying to sweep his campaign finance violation under the rug, claiming it was a mere "bookkeeping issue." Here's the problem with that rationale... either he's straight-up lying or he's such an incompetent leader that a $125,000 accounting error fell through the cracks. Either way, the constituents of AZ-01 are NOT getting the representation they deserve.
We desperately need to restore trust between Congressmembers and the constituents they represent. And the only way to do that is to elect leaders who will have a honest, pragmatic approach in Congress to help Arizonans move forward.
Donate now to help me make sure that happens as I run to unseat David Schweikert this November >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/delina-disanto-for-congress-1?refcode=X_021822_EthicsFollowUp]

subject: Will you help us make history?
body:
|
---

name: Lizzie Pannill Fletcher
subject: One Year Later: Lessons from the Texas Winter Storm
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpfe220218?refcode=lpfe220218_m [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpfe220218?refcode=lpfe220218_m]
Friend,
This week, we are marking the one-year anniversary of the devastating winter storm that left 11 million people across Texas, including me and my family, without power and water for days and with damage everywhere for months. During those freezing days and nights, 246 Texans — from age 1 to 102 — died of hypothermia, carbon monoxide poisoning, fires from trying to keep warm, and car accidents. It was absolutely devastating for all who lived through it — and we see this week that we are still trying to heal.
I said it then, and it remains as true as ever today, that the Texas winter storm showed us just how much leadership matters.
We saw a failure of leadership immediately, as state leaders rushed to deflect blame. In the year since, we have seen that failure of leadership again and again. Despite promises to fix the grid, those responsible for doing so did little of consequence. The Texas Legislature passed a law that looked like it required winterization for this critical infrastructure — but it also allowed companies to opt out of doing so at the same time. Regulations requiring weatherization are stalled. Agencies tasked with oversight have done little to investigate or mitigate.
But we also saw real leadership. People worked around the clock to get others to safety, to get them information, to get the heat back on. I worked with many of our local leaders — from City Council to County Commissioners — to distribute water, and supplies, and reliable information and worked with leaders of all kinds offering help.
This week’s anniversary coincided with another important event: the start of early voting here in Texas. And it is fitting because another lesson of the storm is so important this year: elections matter.
Through the monumental events of the last several years, we all have seen how much who we elect to lead our communities and our country shapes us all. We have seen those elected leaders who have risen to the many challenges we face with compassion and community in mind. And we have seen those who fail to do so. Who we elect matters.
And that brings me to one more reminder this week: You matter. Electing those who lead with head and heart, who help and heal, who unify and inspire takes a coalition. It takes all kinds of work, all the time. And it can’t happen without you.
As we head into the 2022 midterms, the stakes could not be higher. I am grateful to you for being a part of this team, and excited about what we can accomplish this year in Texas and across the country.
Together, we can do anything,
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/sigblock.png]
Donate [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpfe220218?refcode=lpfe220218_m]
PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS
Lizzie Fletcher for Congress
3262 Westheimer Road, PMB 636
Houston, TX 77098
United States

name: Tina Kotek
subject: Bold and innovative is the way forward
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek P
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41960313/331059199/69228752?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MTE4NDNmMy1lYzkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lFqF32ssg9Dra8xKeyNTDvjaxbtwdaiBkuw9xcRZajk=&emci=6780501e-a08a-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&emdi=811843f3-ec90-ec11-a507-281878b83d8a&ceid=15353729)
---
Peter,

Earlier this month I was in Salem to tour the ARCHES Inn, an effort led by the Mid-Willamette Valley Community Action Agency to convert a motel to a transitional shelter for individuals needing services and stability as their first step to finding permanent housing. The ARCHES Inn wouldn't have happened without state support through Project Turnkey.

I made sure Project Turnkey happened in Oregon. Modeled after an idea started in California, Project Turnkey is an innovative approach to providing more shelter space more quickly by helping community organizations acquire motels and hotels to convert them into shelters and transitional housing. When fully finished, the ARCHES Inn in Salem will house 80 to 100 people as a transition to permanent housing.

Because I fought for the money and the public-private partnership to make it happen, Project Turnkey created **19 new shelters in 13 counties** with **865 new units** around the state. **In just 7 months,** this work increased the state's shelter/transitional housing capacity **by 20%!**
| **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41960318/331059205/-2010496638?refcode=em22_220218_arches&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MTE4NDNmMy1lYzkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lFqF32ssg9Dra8xKeyNTDvjaxbtwdaiBkuw9xcRZajk=)**
---

As I walked through the new shelter, I was excited and kept thinking - if we're going to make a real difference in our state's housing crisis, we need more ideas like Project Turnkey.

**We can't keep using the same ideas and expect different results.** We need to be bold to meet the challenges in front of us. I'm in this work to solve problems and make things better.

**[With your support we can continue our work to solve the housing crisis in Oregon. Can you donate $5 or whatever you're able to today>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41960322/331059209/-2010496638?refcode=em22_220218_arches&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MTE4NDNmMy1lYzkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lFqF32ssg9Dra8xKeyNTDvjaxbtwdaiBkuw9xcRZajk=)**
**[DONATE NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41960325/331059212/-2010496638?refcode=em22_220218_arches&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MTE4NDNmMy1lYzkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lFqF32ssg9Dra8xKeyNTDvjaxbtwdaiBkuw9xcRZajk=) **

clean_body: | Peter, Earlier this month I was in Salem to tour the ARCHES Inn, an effort led by the Mid-Willamette Valley Community Action Agency to convert a motel to a transitional shelter for individuals needing services and stability as their first step to finding permanent housing. The ARCHES Inn wouldn't have happened without state support through Project Turnkey. I made sure Project Turnkey happened in Oregon. Modeled after an idea started in California, Project Turnkey is an innovative approach to providing more shelter space more quickly by helping community organizations acquire motels and hotels to convert them into shelters and transitional housing. When fully finished, the ARCHES Inn in Salem will house 80 to 100 people as a transition to permanent housing. Because I fought for the money and the public-private partnership to make it happen, Project Turnkey created 19 new shelters in 13 counties with 865 new units around the state. In just 7 months, this work increased the state's shelter/transitional housing capacity by 20%! | = As I walked through the new shelter, I was excited and kept thinking - if we're going to make a real difference in our state's housing crisis, we need more ideas like Project Turnkey. We can't keep using the same ideas and expect different results. We need to be bold to meet the challenges in front of us. I'm in this work to solve problems and make things better. = = Thank you for your continued support. Let's do this! – Tina Kotek Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek P.O. Box 42307 Portland, OR 97242 This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. You can .

body:
Peter,
We’re over halfway through the month, which means we have to take a hard look at our finances. **We’re doing well, but we need to do better if we’re going to stay on track to reach our second monthly goal of Election Year.** I know you probably get a lot of asks for donations, but here’s why ours is different:
**My opponent Jay Obernolte has millions and millions of dollars to his name, which means he can self-finance his campaign,** thanks to the very same problematic campaign finance rules that we’ll take on once I’m in Congress.
Millionaires and billionaires shouldn’t be able to self-fund campaigns or accept hundreds of thousands of dollars from corporate special elections. **Money influences every aspect of our elections, and that’s why it’s so important that we can raise a competitive amount from our own grassroots base. **
**[Will you chip in today and contribute to our monthly goal? It’s crucial that we can reach our target and challenge Obernolte on a more level playing field. Our average amount given is just $30–will you match it today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41960489/331059443/-431919476?refcode=2-17&amount=30&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODEyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MzE4NDNmMy1lYzkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tkDJ6X-dD_qd4TTJxYTqnplZLt0546vW4lvIX2yJmpw=)**

We know that raising a competitive amount of resources won’t be easy, but we’re already proving we have what it takes.**We’ve worked incredibly hard and raised over $400,000 to date on a 100% people-powered campaign.**
We’re so close to our goal, and reaching it would mean we stay on track for our 2022 budget. Why’s that so important? Because everything that will help us win this race—from field to political to comms—costs money, and a lot of it.**We need to stay strong.**
**[If you can spare a donation right now, your contribution could help us reach our goal. Will you consider contributing our average amount of just $30 right now?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/41960491/331059445/-431919476?refcode=2-17&amount=30&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODEyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MzE4NDNmMy1lYzkwLWVjMTEtYTUwNy0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tkDJ6X-dD_qd4TTJxYTqnplZLt0546vW4lvIX2yJmpw=)**

**Derek Marshall**

name: Aadland For Colorado
subject: Erik does it again!
body:
Second debate wrap up
---
| | | | | | Volume 30 | February 18, 2022
---
|
---
| Erik does it again!
---
| | |
---|---
|
From the Aadland for Congress campaign:

In the second of a series of debates for Republican candidates running in Colorado's Congressional District 7, Erik Aadland proved once again his leadership experience and understanding of the issues make him best suited to serve the people of Colorado.

The forum, hosted by the Jeffco Liberty Coalition, was held in Evergreen Thursday night and featured four of the five candidates running to be the Republican nominee. Candidate Brad Dempsey was not present.

As a decorated combat veteran, Erik showed his expertise in foreign policy and national security, especially at a time when all eyes are focused on Russia's next move at the Ukraine border.

"We have a president that is bringing us to the brink of war," Erik said. "We must get our military back in order... and Congress must keep the president in check."

But his knowledge is not limited to the military. Erik spoke of eradicating wasteful government spending and subsequent taxation which is crippling America's economy. That includes reducing the size of government.

"We need to strike the balance between taxation and freedom. The government should provide basic services--national defense and providing infrastructure," said Erik. "To think we can't have government is irrational--but we need a government that functions efficiently, and we must address the bureaucracy that is extremely bloated and wasteful."

One of many concerns on the minds of Coloradans is that of the high price of health care and its accessibility. Erik showed how Democratic efforts to socialize medicine will only lead to disaster. He supports smart legislation to modify extreme measures.

"Socialized medicine drives up cost and reduces the quality of care. And it makes care harder to get," he said. "We need free market forces with smart balanced government regulations to ensure the quality of care and low prices. We want access, and we want to attract high quality doctors. We also want to show compassion to those who are struggling."

Finally, Erik addressed criticism by another candidate who called petitioning onto the primary ballot "buying signatures." Erik said the best path to win the election and restore a conservative voice to Congressional District 7 is first to simply qualify for the primary by connecting with voters.

"Petitioning on the ballot is purely a strategy. I want the caucus to work and function efficiently, but with petitioning, I connect with the constituents of CD7 who are not participating in the process," he explained. "Petitioning is not buying signatures; it's connecting with voters. This is a call of duty. I'm ready to turn in my signatures and get on the ballot. I don't want to fight with my fellow Republicans. I'm focused on the Democratic opponent."

Throughout the evening, audience members showed appreciation for Erik's thoughtful answers through an abundance of applause. The campaign now looks forward to more vigorous debate next Thursday, Feb. 24 in Broomfield as the North Metro Conservatives host their debate beginning at 6:30 p.m.

name: Wesley Hunt
subject: I am Honored by President Trump's Endorsement!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hunt for Congress
body:
Friend,
The overwhelming support our campaign has received since we launched in
July has been absolutely astonishing. I am blessed and honored to be running
for Congress in TX-38, and I cannot thank all my generous supporters enough for
the time and resources you have dedicated to our campaign. The grassroots
support has been truly incredible.

Throughout the last 7 months, we have also been able to secure the support
from key national conservatives including Senator Ted Cruz, Senator Tim Scott,
Secretary Mike Pompeo, Ambassador Nikki Haley, and so many others. Their
support has been instrumental in our success.

And as of yesterday, we have now earned the endorsement of President Donald J.
Trump! I am absolutely honored to have President Trump's endorsement because he
understands that putting America first is the only way to preserve freedom and
opportunity for future generations.

As your Congressman, I will fight tirelessly to ensure true conservative
values are the legacy we leave for our children, and our children's children.

Sincerely,
Wesley Hunt

Paid for by Hunt for Congress. Military images and information do not imply
endorsement by DOD [Department of Defense] or any service branch.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Click here to unsubscribe
<http://links.wesleyfortexas.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=fd5d6e7aa0334e7ebb096a39dc496016&_s=66e2e7b1411c4512a1f9844ff4a3d8df&_t=RKIohz4hkasQc_x1psz9jNHTIBiizljaiwKlEKz5nSOkmxer_pn4q1QLkVbnEnthou6Vryxr6TP8wbyYjzFeGljQXt4lLOQ5YfOJ7ZXbGOrjPrNeCa0skMvIqYg21TdMH8rp3nXtUOrg9_6NjLh3_TMOYWKeTToCPBiXuxqlRng%3D>
.

Loren Culp has a long record of spreading conspiracy theories about COVID-19, peddling in dangerous demagoguery, and promoting the Big Lie. That means he’ll be joining the ranks of Lauren Boebert and Marjorie Taylor Greene if he ever makes it to Congress.

We must win this race – we just can’t afford to lose Eastern Washington to Cathy McMorris Rodgers and Loren Culp!

Thanks for joining me in this fight. With GOP extremists and insurrectionists rallying behind Trump’s hand-picked candidate, we’ll need all the help we can get in the weeks. So let’s get out there and show them we’re ready to flip this seat blue.

– Doug White

| PAID FOR BY DOUG WHITE FOR CONGRESS

United States

Collin County District Attorney Greg Willis is a strong leader for Collin County. DA Willis ensures our families and communities are safe and secure, and that justice is served. I am honored to have his endorsement for re-election.
